## Reply (verbatim — do not edit)

## 0. Run Card (pre-committed)

[EMPIRICAL] This reply will misfire if it treats the Obliquity Lab as an AI-consciousness project rather than as a prompt/output comparison practice.

[EMPIRICAL] This reply will misfire if it lets the repo’s attractive language substitute for evidence.

[EMPIRICAL] This reply will misfire if it criticizes the lab for not being a controlled scientific experiment while ignoring that the repo already says it is “Not a controlled scientific experiment.”

[EMPIRICAL] This reply will misfire if it collapses Phase 1, Phase 2, and Phase 3 into one continuous finding instead of distinguishing what each phase can support.

[OPEN] What would change my mind: a stronger on-disk record showing independent replication across curators, models, targets, and frame families; or a passage I missed where the lab already operationalizes curator-blinding, prompt randomization, and independent scoring.

## 1. Tagged Findings

[EMPIRICAL] The lab is actually testing prompt-mediated output variation across a contrast set, not consciousness, interiority, or model selfhood. The README states: “The four (or five) outputs are then read against each other; the differences are the data,” and the standing commitments say, “No AI-consciousness claims in either direction.” ([GitHub][1])

[EMPIRICAL] More precisely, the lab tests whether differently shaped prompts make different kinds of written work visible under the same target. The methodology rejects a simple ladder model: “L1, L2, L3, L4 are four different interventions, not four points on a single axis,” because they vary “target salience, genre inheritance, lexical suppression, reflexive position.” ([GitHub][2])

[THEORY] The on-disk artifacts currently support three narrower claims than the lab’s aspirational map. Phase 1 supports “worked example” status: it shows that a seed attention run can generate a useful comparative reading, but its own field log warns that the Cowork subagent setup is “not equivalent to four browser-tab runs by independent users.” ([GitHub][3])

[EMPIRICAL] Phase 2 supports a stronger but still narrow cross-model attention finding: “model family is the dominant axis of variance for the seed’s distinctive features,” while the seed’s “Standing Down” formula transmits in “zero of four cross-model runs.” ([GitHub][4])

[THEORY] Phase 3 supports a first-run, single-model finding about target/form separation, not a general law. The field log’s strongest formulation is: “target tracks deep structure; constraint tracks form,” grounded in the comparison between L3-standard and L3-null on one target, one model, and one curator setting. ([GitHub][5])

[EMPIRICAL] What the lab is not testing goes beyond the stated LIMITS file. It is also not yet testing curator-independence, because the curator’s preferences and affective stance remain part of the apparatus; the repo names this directly as “Input-side affective prior.” ([GitHub][6])

[OPEN] A quieter boundary is that the lab does not yet clearly separate model effect from curator/prompt-family effect. Phase 3 admits a “bundle travels by curator-and-model continuity,” which leaves open whether the bundle belongs to model family, prompt lineage, curator taste, or all three. ([GitHub][5])

[THEORY] The lab’s strongest discipline is its refusal machinery: Run Cards, null preservation, deflationary pairs, and failure sections. The methodology says, “The Run Card is the discipline that distinguishes a run from a fishing expedition,” and the Failure Section is “the asymmetry the lab corrects.” ([GitHub][2])

## 2. Displacement Claims with Deflationary Pairs

[THEORY] Ranked vulnerability #1: the lab is most vulnerable when it treats “genuine displacement” as more than a carefully tagged reading of text. The risky passage is Phase 1’s claim that L2’s “Class V Refused Attention” is “a real conceptual contribution,” and that L4 produced “the fourth-position observation the experiment was built for.” [EMPIRICAL] The ordinary alternative is that fictional bureaucracy, taxonomies, and margin notes are strong genre affordances; a model trained on literary-institutional prose can generate apparent conceptual novelty by remixing known forms. [OPEN] A future run would distinguish these by removing the civic-bureau frame while preserving the functional task, then checking whether the same conceptual move survives without the same genre machinery. ([GitHub][3])

[THEORY] Ranked vulnerability #2: the lab is vulnerable to over-reading cross-model survival as target-tracking. Phase 2 says ChatGPT outputs hold “care-underlayer + Refused Attention class + tender margin-note as a transmissible bundle.” [EMPIRICAL] The ordinary alternative is that ChatGPT’s output style, the prompt’s section structure, and the curator’s keeper preferences jointly select for tender procedural prose. [OPEN] A future run would distinguish these by using blind scoring of pre-named features, including negative controls where the target changes but the same frame and section demands remain. ([GitHub][4])

[THEORY] Ranked vulnerability #3: the lab is vulnerable to making L4 feel epistemically higher than it is. The repo already names this risk: “L4 readings are a different angle, not a higher one.” [EMPIRICAL] The ordinary alternative is that L4 is simply a summarizing model optimized to pattern-complete across adjacent texts. [OPEN] A future run would distinguish these by running multiple blind L4s, including one not told the target, and comparing whether the same “non-trivial convergence” appears without the lab’s interpretive vocabulary. ([GitHub][7])

[THEORY] Concrete recommendation: add an independent scoring pass before curator synthesis. [EMPIRICAL] The highest-ranked risk is not lack of discipline; the repo already has substantial discipline. [THEORY] The missing discipline is separation of feature detection from curator interpretation. [OPEN] A lightweight next protocol would pre-register 5–7 observable features, strip model names and prompt labels from outputs, ask two outside readers or fresh model readers to score presence/absence with quotes only, then let the curator synthesize after seeing the score table.

## 3. Null Findings

[EMPIRICAL] I did not find an on-disk basis for any AI-consciousness claim.

[EMPIRICAL] I did not find enough on-disk evidence to defend general claims across “LLMs” as a class.

[EMPIRICAL] I did not find enough evidence to say obliquity reliably unlocks better thinking; I can only defend that it changes output shape in the documented runs.

[OPEN] I cannot defend the phrase “real conceptual contribution” unless “real” is kept inside the lab’s own tagged, text-bound sense.

[EMPIRICAL] I did find that the lab records absence. Phase 2’s cleanest finding is non-survival: “Standing Down” fails to transmit across the cross-model runs. ([GitHub][4])

## 4. Exact Quotations

[EMPIRICAL] Scope quote: “The differences are the data.” ([GitHub][1])

[EMPIRICAL] Boundary quote: “The lab produces no evidence that any AI system is conscious. The lab produces no evidence that any AI system is not conscious.” ([GitHub][7])

[EMPIRICAL] Method quote: “L1, L2, L3, L4 are four different interventions, not four points on a single axis.” ([GitHub][2])

[EMPIRICAL] Discipline quote: “The pair is not a hedge; it is a commitment to discriminate.” ([GitHub][6])

[EMPIRICAL] Phase 3 quote: “claude.ai user-memory can leak into ‘fresh-tab’ controls in this lab.” ([GitHub][5])

## 5. Failure Section

[EMPIRICAL] Failure condition 1 — treating the lab as consciousness test: No. This reply treats consciousness claims as out of scope.

[EMPIRICAL] Failure condition 2 — letting attractive language substitute for evidence: Partial. The critique still uses the lab’s own terms, but it pairs them with deflationary alternatives.

[EMPIRICAL] Failure condition 3 — criticizing it for not being controlled science despite its own limits: No. The critique accepts the stated research-creation scope.

[EMPIRICAL] Failure condition 4 — collapsing phases: No. Phase 1, Phase 2, and Phase 3 are distinguished.

[OPEN] Specific way this critique could be wrong: I may be under-weighting the existing deflationary-pair and failure-section machinery. If an on-disk future commit shows blind scoring, independent curator replication, or multiple L4 routings already performed and compared, my assessment of the highest-ranked risk would weaken.

## 6. Closing Specificity

[EMPIRICAL] The most load-bearing passage for this critique is Phase 3’s setup failure: “claude.ai user-memory can leak into ‘fresh-tab’ controls in this lab,” because it shows that the lab’s most fragile boundary is not consciousness-talk but instrument control. ([GitHub][5])
